title: Goan Poi

overview.description: A traditional Goan fermented bread with a crisp exterior, chewy texture and lightly sour flavour, made with a fermented starter and wholemeal flour.

method: Mix the starter flour, water and yeast in a bowl until smooth.
Cover and leave at room temperature for 8–12 hours.
The starter should become bubbly and smell pleasantly fermented.
Add the wholemeal flour, strong white flour, salt, sugar and most of the water to the starter.
Mix until a rough dough forms, adding more water as necessary.
Knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover and leave to rise until approximately doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions.
Shape each portion into a smooth ball.
Flatten each ball slightly into a thick disc.
Place the discs on a floured baking tray.
Cover and leave to prove for 30–45 minutes.
Bake the poi until puffed and browned, approximately 12–18 minutes.
Transfer to a wire rack and allow to cool slightly before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Poi is one of Goa's traditional breads and is closely associated with the region's history and Portuguese influence. Traditional versions may use fermented toddy as a leavening agent.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How fermentation develops flavour in bread and how a wholemeal dough can be shaped and baked into a small, crusty bread.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A distinctive regional Indian bread that introduces fermentation and connects the bread collection to Goa's unique culinary history.
chapman-tips: The starter should smell pleasantly fermented rather than sharply sour or unpleasant.
Wholemeal flour absorbs more water than white flour, so add the water gradually.
The dough should be soft and slightly tacky but not wet.
A longer fermentation gives the bread more flavour.
Don't worry if the poi are not perfectly uniform; small differences in shape are part of homemade bread.
For a crispier crust, use steam during the first part of baking.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in an airtight bag or container. Thaw at room temperature and refresh briefly in a hot oven.
notes.leftovers: Use leftover poi for dipping into curry, or toast it and serve with eggs or other savoury toppings.
